word: H6131 - עָקַר - ʻâqar - aw-kar' - Dig down

sections.paragraph:
Original: עקר

Transliteration: ‛âqar

Phonetic: aw-kar'

BDB Definition:

  1. to pluck up, root up
    1. (Qal) to pluck up, root up
    2. (Niphal) to be plucked up
  2. to cut, hamstring
    1. (Piel) to cut, hamstring

Origin: a primitive root

TWOT entry: 1681,1682

Part(s) of speech: Verb
